nodes.label: Accessible Brand Expression
nodes.rationale: Brand colors, motion, logos, and text treatments must preserve WCAG AA contrast, reduced-motion support, and legibility.

nodes.label: Client-Side OCR Rationale
nodes.rationale: OCR runs in the browser to offload server compute, improve scalability, lower cost, and provide immediate feedback.

nodes.label: Avoid Server OCR Complexity
nodes.rationale: Moving Tesseract to the browser avoids worker-file, Docker networking, SSL certificate, and inter-container fetch failures.

nodes.label: Lazy D1 Database Proxy
nodes.rationale: Database initialization is deferred because Cloudflare request context is unavailable at module scope and eager initialization crashes the isolate.

nodes.label: Extend the Generic Shop System
nodes.rationale: Custom event pages should reuse cart and order infrastructure while overriding presentation and checkout to reduce duplication and preserve admin compatibility.

nodes.label: Client-Side OCR
nodes.rationale: Browser-side Tesseract processing reduces server load and keeps receipt analysis on the user's device, while allowing checkout to continue when extraction fails.

nodes.label: Dual-Format Receipt Extraction
nodes.rationale: Images use flexible client OCR for single receipts, while coordinate-based server parsing gives higher accuracy and multi-transaction support for GCash PDF histories.

nodes.label: Dual-Endpoint Object Storage
nodes.rationale: Persisted public URLs must remain browser-accessible, but server-side OCR inside Docker needs an internal service hostname and usually HTTP transport.

nodes.label: Remote Database Caching
nodes.rationale: Aggressive short-lived caching and connection pooling reduce expensive network round trips to a remote PostgreSQL server under concurrent load.

nodes.label: Full-Replacement Spreadsheet Sync
nodes.rationale: Clearing and rewriting the sheet on each run favors a simple, accurate mirror of order state over incremental reconciliation.

nodes.label: Composition Over Flags
nodes.rationale: Separate module tables preserve type-safe foreign keys, keep configuration SQL-queryable, map cleanly to module-specific roles, and make future modules additive.

nodes.label: Code Page Precedence
nodes.rationale: Developer-deployed pages override CMS pages, with CMS as fallback and 404 otherwise, enabling bespoke event experiences without eliminating editable content.

nodes.label: Per-Event Role Grants
nodes.rationale: Module-specific grants and an overseer role replace coarse global flags so authority follows an event and its enabled modules.

nodes.label: Staged Event Architecture Cutover
nodes.rationale: Additive tables, preserved identifiers, manual audit, dual-read compatibility, and a 48-hour delay before destructive cleanup reduce migration and rollback risk without feature flags.

nodes.label: Open Font Collaboration
nodes.author: The Lexend Project Authors
nodes.rationale: The OFL promotes collaborative font development and permits use, modification, embedding, and redistribution while preserving licensing and reserved-name conditions.

nodes.label: Approachable, Competent, Visible Council
nodes.rationale: The council should be approachable without hesitation, competent, visible, able to grow, and supportive of the dorm community.

nodes.label: Context, Inclusivity, Visibility, Competency, and Culture
nodes.norm_label: context, inclusivity, visibility, competency, and culture

nodes.label: Logo Integrity
nodes.rationale: Logos must retain their original proportions, colors, full visibility, and require Branding and Communications approval for alternative use.

nodes.label: Avenir, Proxima Nova, Georgia Pro, and Lexend Deca
nodes.norm_label: avenir, proxima nova, georgia pro, and lexend deca

nodes.label: In ARSA, It’s Good to Be Home
nodes.norm_label: in arsa, it’s good to be home

nodes.label: Home-Centered ARSA Identity
nodes.rationale: The motto positions ARSA and dorm life around belonging, familiarity, and home.

nodes.label: Photo Style Context for Documentation Teams
nodes.rationale: A visual or written photo style gives the documentation team clear expectations for event coverage.
nodes.norm_label: photo style context for documentation teams

nodes.label: Online, Physical, Video, Photo, and Calendar Outputs
nodes.norm_label: online, physical, video, photo, and calendar outputs

nodes.label: Prepublication Brand Approval
nodes.rationale: The final event brandbook must be screened and approved by Branding and Communications at least three weeks before the first post.

nodes.label: Dorm-as-Home Theme Requirement
nodes.rationale: Event themes should connect to the dorm as a home for ARSAn dormers and may refer to the ARSA vision and mission.
